member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.IListenerConfiguration`1.PartitionProcessingByGroupId(Wolverine.Configuration.PartitionSlots)
member.summary: Creates a policy of partitioning the processing of incoming messages by the specified number of slots. Use this to group messages to prevent concurrent processing of messages with the same GroupId while allowing parallel work across GroupIds. The number of "slots" reflects the maximum number of parallel messages that can be handled concurrently

member.summary: "Pin" this endpoint so that it is only active on the leader node

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression.To(System.Uri)
member.summary: All matching records are to be sent to the configured subscriber by Uri

member.summary: Send all the matching messages to the designated Uri string

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression.ToLocalQueue(System.String)
member.summary: Publish the designated message types to the named local queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression.Locally
member.summary: Publishes the matching messages locally to the default local queue

member.name: T:Wolverine.Configuration.IReturnVariableActionSource
member.summary: Given a return variable from a handler method, return a Frame for how Wolverine should handle that return value

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.UseDurableOutbox
member.summary: Force any messages enqueued to this worker queue to be durable by enrolling outgoing messages in the active, durable envelope outbox

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.BufferedInMemory
member.summary: By default, messages on this worker queue will not be persisted until being successfully handled

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.CustomizeOutgoing(System.Action{Wolverine.Envelope})
member.summary: Apply envelope customization rules to any outgoing messages to this endpoint

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.CustomizeOutgoingMessagesOfType``1(System.Action{Wolverine.Envelope})
member.summary: Apply envelope customization rules to any outgoing messages to this endpoint only for messages of either type TMessage or types that implement or inherit from TMessage

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.CustomizeOutgoingMessagesOfType``1(System.Action{Wolverine.Envelope,``0})
member.summary: Apply envelope customization rules to any outgoing messages to this endpoint only for messages of either type TMessage or types that implement or inherit from TMessage

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.DeliverWithin(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Limit all outgoing messages to a certain "deliver within" time span after which the messages will be discarded even if not successfully delivered or processed

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.CircuitBreaking(System.Action{Wolverine.Configuration.ICircuitParameters})
member.summary: Fine-tune the circuit breaker parameters for this outgoing subscriber endpoint

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.SendInline
member.summary: Outgoing messages are sent to the transport completely inline in a predictable way with no retries

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.GlobalSender
member.summary: In the case of being part of tenancy aware group of message transports, this setting makes this listening endpoint a "global" endpoint that does not conform to the tenant-specific topology.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ISubscriberConfiguration`1.ConfigureSending(System.Action{Wolverine.ErrorHandling.SendingFailurePolicies})
member.summary: Configure failure handling policies for outgoing message send failures on this specific endpoint. These rules take priority over global policies.
member.param: Action to configure the sending failure policies

member.name: T:Wolverine.Configuration.IWolverinePolicy
member.summary: Marker interface to tell Wolverine that yes, this is *some* sort of wolverine policy

member.name: T:Wolverine.Configuration.IWolverineReturnType
member.summary: Just a marker type that designates that this type has some kind of specific return handling meaning and should not be used as a response

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.InteroperableListenerConfiguration`4.UseInterop(`2)
member.summary: Use a custom interoperability strategy to map Wolverine messages to an upstream system's protocol

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.InteroperableListenerConfiguration`4.UseInterop(System.Action{`1,`3})
member.summary: Customize the basic envelope mapping for interoperability. This mechanism is suitable if you are mostly needing to modify how headers are communicated from and to external systems through the underlying transport

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.InteroperableListenerConfiguration`4.UseInterop(System.Func{Wolverine.Runtime.IWolverineRuntime,`1,`2})
member.summary: Create a completely customized mapper using the WolverineRuntime and the current Endpoint. This is built lazily at system bootstrapping time

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.InteroperableListenerConfiguration`4.InteropWithCloudEvents(System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Interop with upstream systems by reading messages with the CloudEvents specification

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.PartitionProcessingByGroupId(Wolverine.Configuration.PartitionSlots)
member.summary: Creates a policy of sharding the processing of incoming messages by the specified number of slots. Use this to group messages to prevent concurrent processing of messages with the same GroupId while allowing parallel work across GroupIds. The number of "slots" reflects the maximum number of parallel messages that can be handled concurrently

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.GlobalListener
member.summary: In the case of being part of tenancy aware group of message transports, this setting makes this listening endpoint a "global" endpoint rather than a tenant id aware endpoint that spans multiple message brokers.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.ListenOnlyAtLeader
member.summary: "Pin" this endpoint so that it is only active on the leader node

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.ExclusiveNodeWithParallelism(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Configure this listener to run exclusively on a single node in the cluster, but allow parallel message processing within that node. This is useful for scenarios where you need single-node consistency but want to maintain throughput.
member.param: Maximum number of messages to process in parallel on the exclusive node. Default is 10.
Optional endpoint name for identification
member.returns: The configuration for method chaining

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.ExclusiveNodeWithSessionOrdering(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Configure this listener to run exclusively on a single node with parallel processing, but maintain ordering within specific groups (sessions). This is particularly useful for Azure Service Bus with sessions or similar scenarios.
member.param: Maximum number of sessions/groups to process in parallel. Default is 10.
Optional endpoint name for identification
member.returns: The configuration for method chaining

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.ListenerCount(System.Int32)
member.summary: To optimize the message listener throughput, start up multiple listening endpoints. This is most necessary when using inline processing

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.DefaultIncomingMessage``1
member.summary: Assume that any unidentified, incoming message types is the type "T". This is primarily for interoperability with non-Wolverine applications

member.name: M:Wolverine.Configuration.ListenerConfiguration`2.DefaultIncomingMessage(System.Type)
member.summary: Assume that any unidentified, incoming message types is the type "T". This is primarily for interoperability with non-Wolverine applications

member.summary: For endpoints that send or receive messages in batches, this governs the maximum number of messages that will be received or sent in one batch

member.name: T:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions
member.summary: Optional customizations and metadata for how a message should be delivered

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.Headers
member.summary: Optional metadata about this message

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.DeliverBy
member.summary: Instruct Wolverine to throw away this message if it is not successfully sent and processed by the time specified

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.AckRequested
member.summary: Is an acknowledgement requested

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.ScheduledTime
member.summary: Used by scheduled jobs or transports with a native scheduled send functionality to have this message processed by the receiving application at or after the designated time

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.DeliverWithin
member.summary: Set the DeliverBy property to have this message thrown away if it cannot be sent before the allotted time

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.ScheduleDelay
member.summary: Set the ScheduleTime to now plus the value of the supplied TimeSpan

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.ResponseType
member.summary: Declare that this application is interested in receiving a response of this message type upon message receipt

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.SagaId
member.summary: If this message is part of a stateful saga, this property identifies the underlying saga state object

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.TenantId
member.summary: Override the tenant id metadata for a single envelope

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.ContentType
member.summary: Mimetype of the serialized data

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.RoutingInformation
member.summary: Extra routing information to send to the eventual Wolverine transport. Mostly built for WebSockets

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.GroupId
member.summary: Application defined message group identifier. Part of AMQP 1.0 spec as the "group-id" property. Session identifier for Azure Service Bus. MessageGroupId for Amazon SQS FIFO Queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.DeduplicationId
member.summary: MessageDeduplicationId for Amazon SQS FIFO Queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.PartitionKey
member.summary: Partition Key when sending via Kafka

member.name: M:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.WithHeader(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Add a header key/value pair to the outgoing message

member.name: M:Wolverine.DeliveryOptions.RequireResponse``1
member.summary: Shortcut to build DeliveryOptions requiring the specified response type

member.name: F:Wolverine.DurabilityMode.Solo
member.summary:
The durability agent will be optimized to run in a single node. This is very useful for local development where you may be frequently stopping and restarting the service
All known agents will automatically start on the local node. The recovered inbox/outbox messages will start functioning immediately

member.name: F:Wolverine.DurabilityMode.Balanced
member.summary: Normal mode that assumes that Wolverine is running on multiple load balanced nodes with messaging active

member.name: F:Wolverine.DurabilityMode.Serverless
member.summary: Disables all message persistence to optimize Wolverine for usage within serverless functions like AWS Lambda or Azure Functions. Requires that all endpoints be inline

member.name: F:Wolverine.DurabilityMode.MediatorOnly
member.summary: Optimizes Wolverine for usage as strictly a mediator tool. This completely disables all node persistence including the inbox and outbox

member.name: T:Wolverine.MessageIdentity
member.summary: Controls how Wolverine chooses to identify received message uniqueness in message storage

member.name: F:Wolverine.MessageIdentity.IdOnly
member.summary: The default, "classic" behavior where Wolverine only identifies a received message by the unique message id

member.name: F:Wolverine.MessageIdentity.IdAndDestination
member.summary: Make Wolverine identify message identity uniqueness by a combination of the message id and destination (received_at). Use this if you are having a single Wolverine process receive the same message from multiple external listeners. This may be necessary for some "Modular Monolith" approaches

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.TenantIdStyle
member.summary:
For systems that use multi-tenancy, this controls how Wolverine does or does not "correct" the supplied tenant id, with the default behavior being to use case-sensitive tenant ids.
Use the IServiceCollection.CritterStackDefaults() method to change this

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.MessageStorageSchemaName
member.summary: If set, this establishes a default database schema name for all registered message storage databases. Use this with a modular monolith approach where all modules target the same physical database. The default is null.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.Mode
member.summary: Control and optimize the durability agent behavior within Wolverine applications

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.MessageIdentity
member.summary: Direct Wolverine on how it judges message identity. "Classic" default is IdOnly. Switch to IdAndDestination for Modular Monolith usage where you may be receiving the same message and processing separately in different external transport listening endpoints

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.OutboxStaleTime
member.summary: If non-null, this directs Wolverine to "push" any message in the durable outbox that is older than the configured time even if the message is marked as owned by an active node

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.InboxStaleTime
member.summary: If non-null, this directs Wolverine to "push" any message in the durable inbox that is older than the configured time even if the message is marked as owned by an active node. Should NOT ever be necessary, but it's an imperfect world. Enable this if you see "stuck" envelopes

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.EnableInboxPartitioning
member.summary: For persistence mechanisms that support this (PostgreSQL), this directs Wolverine to use partitioning based on the envelope status for the transactional inbox storage. This can be a performance optimization, but does require a database migration if enabled

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.DurabilityAgentEnabled
member.summary: Should the message durability agent be enabled during execution. The default is true.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.KeepAfterMessageHandling
member.summary: How long should successfully handled messages be kept to use in idempotency checking

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.RecoveryBatchSize
member.summary: Governs the page size for how many persisted incoming or outgoing messages will be loaded at one time for attempted retries or scheduled jobs

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.NodeReassignmentPollingTime
member.summary: How frequently Wolverine will attempt to reassign incoming or outgoing persisted methods from nodes that are detected to be offline

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.FirstNodeReassignmentExecution
member.summary: When should the first execution of the node reassignment job execute after application startup.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.MetricsCollectionSamplingInterval
member.summary: Interval between collecting persisted and queued message metrics

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.ScheduledJobFirstExecution
member.summary: How long to wait before the first execution of polling for ready, persisted scheduled messages

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.ScheduledJobPollingTime
member.summary: Polling interval for executing scheduled messages

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.FirstHealthCheckExecution
member.summary: Time span before the first health check is executed

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.HealthCheckPollingTime
member.summary: Polling time between health checks

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.StaleNodeTimeout
member.summary: Age of health check data before a node is considered to be "stale" or dormant and will be recovered by the durability agent

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.CheckAssignmentPeriod
member.summary: How often should Wolverine do a full check that all assigned agents are really running and try to restart (or stop) any differences from the last good set of assignments

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.TenantCheckPeriod
member.summary: If using any kind of dynamic multi-tenancy where Wolverine should discover new tenants, this is the polling time. Default is 5 seconds

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.UpdateMetricsPeriod
member.summary: If using any kind of message persistence, this is the polling time to update the metrics on the persisted envelope counts. Default is 5 seconds

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.DurabilityMetricsEnabled
member.summary: Is the polling for durability metrics enabled? Default is true

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.DeadLetterQueueExpiration
member.summary: If DeadLetterQueueExpirationEnabled is true, this governs how long persisted dead letter queue messages will be retained. The default is 10 days from the time the message is persisted.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.DeadLetterQueueExpirationEnabled
member.summary: Opt-in flag governs whether or not dead letter queued messages have expiration enforced for database stored dead letter messages. The default is false.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.NodeEventRecordExpirationTime
member.summary: Configurable time to keep records in the wolverine_node_records storage (or equivalent) for node records. Default is 5 days

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.SendingAgentIdleTimeout
member.summary: How long a sending agent can be idle before it is considered stale and eligible for cleanup. Default is 5 minutes.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.UseSyncRetryBlock
member.summary: When this option is enabled retry block used in InlineSendingAgent will synchronously wait on sending task to assure the message is send. When set to default behavior is used so InlineSendingAgent agent will try to send a message and when failed it will give control to caller and retry on other thread in async manner

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.NodeAssignmentHealthCheckTracingEnabled
member.summary: Controls whether health check operations emit telemetry traces for 'wolverine_node_assignments'. Default is true to maintain backwards compatibility. Set to false to completely disable health check tracing.

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.NodeAssignmentHealthCheckTraceSamplingPeriod
member.summary: When set, health check traces will be throttled to emit at most once per this time period. For example, set to 10 minutes to only emit traces every 10 minutes instead of every health check. This reduces telemetry volume while still providing periodic visibility. Default is null (no throttling - all health checks are traced when NodeAssignmentHealthCheckTracingEnabled is true).

member.name: P:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.DrainTimeout
member.summary: Maximum time to wait for in-flight message handlers to complete during graceful shutdown before proceeding with the shutdown sequence. Default is 30 seconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.DurabilitySettings.Cancel
member.summary: Get or set the logical Wolverine service name. By default, this is derived from the name of a custom WolverineOptions

member.summary: Instruct Wolverine to throw away this message if it is not successfully sent and processed by the time specified

member.summary: Used by scheduled jobs or transports with a native scheduled send functionality to have this message processed by the receiving application at or after the designated time

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ScheduleDelay
member.summary: Set the ScheduleTime to now plus the value of the supplied TimeSpan. If set, this value is retained just for the sake of testing

member.summary: The raw, serialized message data

member.summary: The actual message to be sent or being received

member.summary: Number of times that Wolverine has tried to process this message. Will reflect the current attempt number

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.SendAttempts
member.summary: Number of times that Wolverine has tried to send this message. This is tracked separately from handler Attempts and is used by sending failure policies.

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.Source
member.summary: The name of the service that sent this envelope

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.MessageType
member.summary: Message type alias for the contents of this Envelope

member.summary: Set the MessageType to Wolverine's message type name for T

member.summary: Set the MessageType to Wolverine's message type name for this message type

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ReplyUri
member.summary: Location where any replies should be sent

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ContentType
member.summary: Mimetype of the serialized data

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.CorrelationId
member.summary: Correlating identifier for the logical workflow or system action

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.SagaId
member.summary: If this message is part of a stateful saga, this property identifies the underlying saga state object

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ConversationId
member.summary: Id of the immediate message or workflow that caused this envelope to be sent

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.Destination
member.summary: Location that this message should be sent

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ParentId
member.summary: The open telemetry activity parent id. Wolverine uses this to correctly correlate connect activity across services

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.TenantId
member.summary: User defined tenant identifier for multi-tenancy strategies. This is part of metrics reporting and message correlation

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.UserName
member.summary: The authenticated user name for tracking and auditing purposes

member.name: F:Wolverine.Envelope.DefaultAcceptedContentTypes
member.summary: Specifies the accepted content types for the requested reply

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.Id
member.summary: Specific message id for this envelope

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ReplyRequested
member.summary: If specified, the message type alias for the reply message that is requested for this message

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.TopicName
member.summary: Designates the topic name for outgoing messages to topic-based publish/subscribe routing. This property is only used for routing

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.EndpointName
member.summary: Purely informational in testing scenarios to record the endpoint this envelope was published to

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.WasPersistedInOutbox
member.summary: Used internally to understand where an envelope is in persisted state

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.GroupId
member.summary: Application defined message group identifier. Part of AMQP 1.0 spec as the "group-id" property. Session identifier for Azure Service Bus. MessageGroupId for Amazon SQS FIFO Queue. This is the Group Id for Kafka consumers, if there is one

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.DeduplicationId
member.summary: MessageDeduplicationId for Amazon SQS FIFO Queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.PartitionKey
member.summary: Key partition for Kafka

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.ScheduleDelayed(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Schedule this envelope to be sent or executed after a delay

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.RoutingInformation
member.summary: Used to "smuggle" contextual information to some messaging transports

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.ScheduleAt(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Schedule this envelope to be sent or executed at a certain time

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.IsScheduledForLater(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Should the processing of this message be scheduled for a later time

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.IsExpired
member.summary: Has this envelope expired according to its DeliverBy value

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.Offset
member.summary: For stream based transports (Kafka/RedPanda, this will reflect the message offset. This is strictly informational

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.Store
member.summary: For some forms of modular monoliths, Wolverine needs to track what message store persisted this envelope for later tracking

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.KeepUntil
member.summary: Marks the time stamp for how long this envelope should be retained as "Handled" in the inbox for idempotency protections

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.WasPersistedInInbox
member.summary: Helps denote to the transactional middleware that this envelope was persisted or not to aid in the "Handled" behavior

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.DoNotCascadeResponse
member.summary: Used internally only, tells Wolverine's cascading message logic to *not* publish the designated response message as a cascading message. Originally added for the Http transport request/reply

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.Status
member.summary: Status according to the message persistence

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.OwnerId
member.summary: Node owner of this message. 0 denotes that no node owns this message

member.name: P:Wolverine.Envelope.ExecutionTime
member.summary: How long did the current execution take?

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.SetMetricsTag(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Add an additional tag for information written to metrics. This is purposely kept separate from open telemetry activity tracking

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.CreateForResponse(System.Object)
member.summary: Create a new Envelope that is a response to the current Envelope

member.name: M:Wolverine.Envelope.IsPing
member.summary: Is this envelope for a "ping" message used by Wolverine to evaluate whether a sending endpoint can be restarted

member.name: T:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions
member.summary: Models circuit breaker mechanics to pause the message processing for a single listener when the designated percentage of messages have failed over a tracking period

member.name: P:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions.MinimumThreshold
member.summary: The minimum number of messages that should be captured by the circuit breaker before applying the failure filter. This prevents the circuit breaker from tripping off on application startup or without enough messages to make an adequate judgement about the health of the system. The default is 10.

member.name: P:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions.FailurePercentageThreshold
member.summary: The percentage of messages failing that will trip off the circuit breaker from 1-100. The default is 15.

member.name: P:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions.PauseTime

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.MoveToErrorQueue
member.summary: Immediately move the message to the error queue when the exception caught matches this criteria

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.Requeue(System.Int32)
member.summary: Requeue the message back to the incoming transport, with the message being dead lettered when the maximum number of attempts is reached
member.param: The maximum number of attempts to process the message. The default is 3

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.RequeueIndefinitely
member.summary: Requeue the message back to the incoming transport no matter how many times the message has failed. Use with caution obviously!!!!!

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.Discard
member.summary: Discard the message without any further attempt to process the message

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.ScheduleRetry(System.TimeSpan[])
member.summary: Schedule the message for additional attempts with a delay. Use this method to effect an "exponential backoff" policy

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.ScheduleRetryIndefinitely(System.TimeSpan[])
member.summary: Schedule the message for additional attempts with a scheduled delay. The last delay will be used indefinitively. Use this method to effect an indefinite "exponential backoff" policy where the message is removed from the queue so other messages can proceed

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.RetryOnce
member.summary: Retry the message processing inline one time

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.RetryTimes(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retry the message the given number attempts without any delay or moving the message back to the original queue
member.param: The number of immediate retries allowed

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.RetryWithCooldown(System.TimeSpan[])
member.summary: Retry message failures a define number of times with user-specified cooldown times between events. This allows for "exponential backoff" strategies

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.PauseThenRequeue(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Pause the current thread for a set amount of time, then requeue the message. This was created specifically to create retries without incurring concurrency issues from subsequent messages being processed simultaneously

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.CustomAction(System.Func{Wolverine.Runtime.IWolverineRuntime,Wolverine.IEnvelopeLifecycle,System.Exception,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.String,System.Nullable{Wolverine.Runtime.Handlers.InvokeResult})
member.summary: Take out an additional, user-defined action upon message failures
member.param: Diagnostic description of the failure action
If specified, this error action will be executed for inline message execution through IMessageBus.InvokeAsync()

member.name: M:Wolverine.ErrorHandling.IFailureActions.CustomActionIndefinitely(System.Func{Wolverine.Runtime.IWolverineRuntime,Wolverine.IEnvelopeLifecycle,System.Exception,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.String,System.Nullable{Wolverine.Runtime.Handlers.InvokeResult})
member.summary: Execute a user-defined action for every attempt until the custom action decides to stop. This allows the custom action to handle its own retry logic and termination conditions.

member.name: P:Wolverine.IMessageContext.CorrelationId

member.name: P:Wolverine.IMessageContext.UserName

member.summary: Opt out of Wolverine's default convention of routing messages to the local node's queues Use this to force messages without explicit routing rules to be sent to external transports even if the node has a message handler for the message type

member.name: M:Wolverine.IPolicies.AutoApplyIdempotencyOnNonTransactionalHandlers
member.summary: Apply eager message idempotency checks to any message handler chains that are not otherwise transactional. Example is a handler that calls an external web service but does not make any changes to the current system's databases or storage

member.summary: Advanced usage to map an open type of an interface for a message to the actual closed type Pretty well built for Marten event publishing

member.name: M:Wolverine.IPolicies.ConventionalLocalRoutingIsAdditive
member.summary: Normally, Wolverine will not apply message routing conventions to message types that the current application handles locally if the local routing conventions apply subscriptions. Enabling this option will make Wolverine additively create subscriptions for a message type both locally and for any external message broker routing conventions that are applied to the system

member.name: P:Wolverine.IPolicies.PublishAgentEvents
member.summary: Should Wolverine try to publish internal events about agent assignment, node history, and endpoint events to the message bus? The default is false;

member.name: M:Wolverine.IPolicies.PropagateGroupIdToPartitionKey
member.summary: Automatically propagate the originating message's GroupId to the PartitionKey of outgoing envelopes. This is useful when using Kafka and you want cascaded messages to inherit the partition key from the originating message's group/stream id without manually specifying DeliveryOptions on every outgoing message.

member.name: P:Wolverine.Persistence.EntityAttribute.MaybeSoftDeleted
member.summary:
Should Wolverine consider soft-deleted entities to be missing if deleted. I.e., if an entity can be found, but is marked as deleted, is this considered a "good" entity and the message handling or HTTP execution should continue?
If the document is soft-deleted, whether the endpoint should receive the document (true) or NULL ( false). Set it to false and combine it with so a 404 will be returned for soft-deleted documents.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Persistence.EntityDefaults
member.summary: Global default settings for entity loading behavior across all [Entity], [Document], [Aggregate], [ReadAggregate], and [WriteAggregate] attributes. Individual attribute settings always take precedence over these defaults.

member.name: P:Wolverine.Persistence.EntityDefaults.OnMissing
member.summary: The default behavior when a required entity is not found. Individual attributes can override this value. Built-in default is .

member.name: P:Wolverine.Persistence.EntityDefaults.MaybeSoftDeleted
member.summary: The default behavior for whether soft-deleted entities should be treated as valid. If false, soft-deleted entities are treated as missing. Individual attributes can override this value. Built-in default is true.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Persistence.EnvelopeGenerator
member.summary: Used to generate fake Envelope data for test harnesses including CritterWatch

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.OnMissing.Simple404
member.summary: Default behavior. In a message handler, the execution will just stop after logging that the data was missing. In an HTTP endpoint the request will stop w/ an empty body and 404 status code

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.OnMissing.ProblemDetailsWith400
member.summary: In a message handler, the execution will log that the required data is missing and stop execution. In an HTTP endpoint the request will stop w/ a 400 response and a ProblemDetails body describing the missing data

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.OnMissing.ProblemDetailsWith404
member.summary: In a message handler, the execution will log that the required data is missing and stop execution. In an HTTP endpoint the request will stop w/ a 404 status code response and a ProblemDetails body describing the missing data

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.OnMissing.ThrowException
member.summary: Throws a RequiredDataMissingException using the MissingMessage

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.IdempotencyStyle.None
member.summary: No additional idempotency checks will be made by Wolverine for this message handler. Only applies to messages received at "Inline" or "Buffered" endpoints

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.IdempotencyStyle.Optimistic
member.summary:
Message idempotency will be checked at the time the current transaction is being committed. This mode is a little more optimal as it allows for more database command batching, but should not be used if the message handling involves any actions not part of the current transaction like calls to external web services
Only applies to messages received at "Inline" or "Buffered" endpoints

member.name: F:Wolverine.Persistence.IdempotencyStyle.Eager
member.summary:
Message idempotency will be checked before any other message handling takes place. This is appropriate for cases where the message handling carries out some kind of action like a call to an external web service that is not part of the current transaction. This does potentially cause extra network round trips to the database
Only applies to messages received at "Inline" or "Buffered" endpoints

member.name: T:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics
member.summary: Immutable snapshot of message handling metrics for a specific message type and destination combination over a time range. Produced by on each sampling period. Contains per-tenant breakdowns of execution counts, effective times, and exception counts.
member.param: The fully-qualified CLR message type name. Set to "*" in results to indicate aggregation across all message types.
The destination endpoint URI. Set to all:// in results to indicate aggregation across all destinations.
The time window this snapshot covers, from the start of accumulation to the export timestamp.
Per-tenant metrics breakdowns. Empty when no messages were processed in the time range.

member.summary: The fully-qualified CLR message type name. Set to "*" in results to indicate aggregation across all message types.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics.Sum(System.String,System.Uri,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics})
member.summary: Combines multiple snapshots into a single aggregate. The time range spans the earliest From to the latest To across all inputs. Per-tenant data is grouped by tenant ID, with execution counts, effective times, and exception counts summed within each tenant.
member.param: The message type to assign to the resulting aggregate.
The destination to assign to the resulting aggregate.
The metrics snapshots to aggregate.
member.returns: A single aggregated snapshot. Returns an empty all-time snapshot if the input is empty.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics.Sum(Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics[])
member.summary: Combines multiple snapshots that share the same message type and destination. Takes the message type and destination from the first element.
member.param: The metrics snapshots to aggregate. Must contain at least one element.
member.returns: A single aggregated snapshot.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics.SumByDestination(Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics[])
member.summary: Groups the input metrics by and sums each group, collating across message types. Each resulting snapshot has its set to "*" to indicate aggregation across all message types for that destination.
member.param: The metrics snapshots to group and aggregate.
member.returns: One aggregated snapshot per unique destination URI.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics.SumByMessageType(Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics[])
member.summary: Groups the input metrics by and sums each group, collating across destinations. Each resulting snapshot has its set to all:// to indicate aggregation across all destinations for that message type.
member.param: The metrics snapshots to group and aggregate.
member.returns: One aggregated snapshot per unique message type.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageHandlingMetrics.Weight(System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiplies every numeric value in this snapshot and all nested records by the given . Returns this unchanged when is 1. Intended for building weighted average calculations where snapshots from different time periods or nodes need proportional scaling before summation.
member.param: The multiplier to apply. Must be a positive integer.
member.returns: The same instance if weight is 1, otherwise a new weighted copy.
member.exception: Thrown when is zero or negative.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.PerTenantMetrics

Handler execution count and total execution time in milliseconds.
Message completion count and total end-to-end time in milliseconds.
Per-exception-type counts of failures and dead letters, ordered alphabetically by type name.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.Executions
member.summary: Handler execution statistics: the number of executions and total wall-clock execution time. Accumulated from data points where each handler invocation increments by one and adds its duration to . Average execution time can be calculated as TotalTime / Count.
member.param: The number of handler executions.
The sum of handler execution durations in milliseconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.Executions.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int64)
member.summary: Handler execution statistics: the number of executions and total wall-clock execution time. Accumulated from data points where each handler invocation increments by one and adds its duration to . Average execution time can be calculated as TotalTime / Count.
member.param: The number of handler executions.

member.summary: The number of handler executions.

member.name: P:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.Executions.TotalTime
member.summary: The sum of handler execution durations in milliseconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.Executions.Weight(System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiplies both and by the given weight.
member.param: The multiplier to apply.
member.returns: A new weighted copy.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.EffectiveTime
member.summary: End-to-end (effective) time statistics: the number of completed messages and their total elapsed time from send to completion. Accumulated from data points where each completion increments by one and adds its elapsed time to . This measures latency from Envelope.SentAt to processing completion, capturing queueing, transport, and handler execution time combined. Average effective time can be calculated as TotalTime / Count.
member.param: The number of messages that completed processing.
The sum of end-to-end elapsed times in milliseconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.EffectiveTime.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Double)
member.summary: End-to-end (effective) time statistics: the number of completed messages and their total elapsed time from send to completion. Accumulated from data points where each completion increments by one and adds its elapsed time to . This measures latency from Envelope.SentAt to processing completion, capturing queueing, transport, and handler execution time combined. Average effective time can be calculated as TotalTime / Count.
member.param: The number of messages that completed processing.
The sum of end-to-end elapsed times in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.EffectiveTime.Count
member.summary: The number of messages that completed processing.

member.name: P:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.EffectiveTime.TotalTime
member.summary: The sum of end-to-end elapsed times in milliseconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.EffectiveTime.Weight(System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiplies both and by the given weight.
member.param: The multiplier to apply.
member.returns: A new weighted copy.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageTypeMetricsAccumulator
member.summary: Accumulates handler metrics for a single message type and destination combination using a batching pipeline. records are posted to which batches them (up to 500 items or 250ms) before applying to the underlying . On each sampling period, snapshots the accumulated counters into an immutable record and resets the counters for the next period.

member.summary: The underlying mutable counter storage for this message type and destination.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MessageTypeMetricsAccumulator.TriggerExport(System.Int32)
member.summary: Snapshots the accumulated counters into an immutable record spanning from to now, then resets the counters and advances to the current time for the next accumulation window. Called by on each sampling period.
member.param: The assigned node number for this Wolverine instance.
member.returns: An immutable metrics snapshot for the completed accumulation window.

member.name: T:Wolverine.Runtime.Metrics.MetricsAccumulator
member.summary: Top-level coordinator for message handling metrics collection. Maintains an immutable collection of instances, one per unique (message type, destination) pair. Runs a background loop that periodically (every WolverineOptions.Metrics.SamplingPeriod, default 5 seconds) triggers each accumulator to export its snapshot via . Only snapshots with at least one tenant data point are published.

member.summary: Creates a new metrics accumulator bound to the given runtime.

Optionally configure each local queue's behavior

member.summary: Configure global partitioning which ensures that no two messages with the same GroupId are ever executed concurrently throughout an application cluster. Both external transport queues and local queues will be forced to durable mode.
member.param: Configure the global partitioned topology

member.summary: Register a custom message grouping rule

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Partitioning.MessagePartitioningRules.ByPropertyNamed(System.String[])
member.summary: Determine the GroupId of a message by looking for a property with a matching name on the message type. This is useful when your message types are auto-generated (e.g. from .proto files) and you cannot add a marker interface. The first matching property name wins. Property values are converted to string via ToString(), with null values becoming string.Empty.
member.param: The property names to look for on each message type

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Partitioning.MessagePartitioningRules.ByMessage(System.Type,System.Reflection.PropertyInfo)
member.summary: Add a grouping rule based on a concrete message type and the property of the message type that exposes the group id information Used extensively internally

member.name: M:Wolverine.Runtime.Partitioning.MessagePartitioningRules.UseInferredMessageGrouping
member.summary: As possible, let Wolverine infer the message grouping based on the message usage. For example, messages related to a Saga will use any saga id property in the command type. In the Marten aggregate handler workflow, Wolverine will use a property on the command type that identifies the event stream *if only one event stream is impacted*

member.summary: Makes so that the convention only applies to outbound messages, and disables discovery of listeners

member.name: M:Wolverine.Transports.MessageRoutingConvention`4.ExcludeTypes(System.Func{System.Type,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Create an deny list of included message types. This is accumulative.

member.name: M:Wolverine.Transports.MessageRoutingConvention`4.QueueNameForListener(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Override the convention for determining the queue name for receiving incoming messages of the message type. Returning null or empty is interpreted as "don't create a new queue for this message type". Default is the MessageTypeName

member.name: M:Wolverine.Transports.MessageRoutingConvention`4.IdentifierForListener(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Override the convention for determining the queue name for receiving incoming messages of the message type. Returning null or empty is interpreted as "don't create a new queue for this message type". Default is the MessageTypeName

member.name: M:Wolverine.Transports.MessageRoutingConvention`4.IdentifierForSender(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Override the convention for determining the destination object name that should receive messages of the message type. Returning null or empty is interpreted as "don't create a new queue for this message type". Default is the MessageTypeName

member.summary: Completely defines and configures a Wolverine application

member.name: F:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.RememberedApplicationAssembly
member.summary: You may use this to "help" Wolverine in testing scenarios to force it to consider this assembly as the main application assembly rather that assuming that the IDE or test runner assembly is the application assembly

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.ApplicationAssembly
member.summary: The main application assembly for this Wolverine system. You may need or want to explicitly set this in automated test harness scenarios. Defaults to the application entry assembly

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.Assemblies
member.summary: All of the assemblies that Wolverine is searching for message handlers and other Wolverine items

member.summary: Configure batch processing of an incoming (or local) message type. Note that Wolverine will require you to use the Array of that element type for the actual batch handler
member.param: Optional fine-tuning of the batch size, triggering, and local queue
member.typeparam: The message type to be batched

member.name: M:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.BatchMessagesOf(System.Type,System.Action{Wolverine.Runtime.Batching.BatchingOptions})
member.summary: Configure batch processing of an incoming (or local) message type. Note that Wolverine will require you to use the Array of that element type for the actual batch handler
member.param: The message type to be batched
Optional fine-tuning of the batch size, triggering, and local queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.EntityDefaults
member.summary: Global default settings for entity loading behavior with [Entity], [Document], [Aggregate], [ReadAggregate], and [WriteAggregate] attributes. Per-attribute settings always take precedence over these defaults.

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.SendingFailure
member.summary: Global failure handling policies for outgoing message send failures. Use this to configure how Wolverine handles exceptions that occur when trying to send messages to external transports.

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.EnableRelayOfUserName
member.summary: When enabled, Wolverine will automatically relay the authenticated user name from HTTP ClaimsPrincipal through the messaging infrastructure, propagating it on outgoing envelopes and into Marten's IDocumentSession.LastModifiedBy

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.ServiceLocationPolicy
member.summary:
What is the policy within this application for whether or not it is valid to allow Service Location within the generated code for message handlers or HTTP endpoints. Default is AllowedByWarn. Just keep in mind that Wolverine really does not want you to use service location if you don't have to!
Please see https://wolverinefx.net/guide/codegen.html for more information

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.MultipleHandlerBehavior
member.summary:
How should Wolverine treat message handlers for the same message type? Default is ClassicCombineIntoOneLogicalHandler, but change this if wanting to execute different handlers for the same type of message in different endpoints.
This frequently comes into play with "modular monolith" architectures

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.MessagePartitioning
member.summary:
Use to establish rules about determining the message GroupId metadata that is used by Wolverine for message sharding or with message transports like Azure Service Bus, AWS SQS, or Kafka that respect some kind of "group id"
This will be automatically applied to all outgoing messages, but will never override any explicitly defined Envelope.GroupId

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.MetadataRules
member.summary: Internal list of IEnvelopeRule instances that are applied via ApplyCorrelation to outgoing envelopes in PersistOrSendAsync

member.name: M:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.AddMessageHandler``1(Wolverine.Runtime.Handlers.MessageHandler{``0})
member.summary: For advanced usages, this gives you the ability to register pre-canned message handling that does not require any code generation.

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.CodeGeneration
member.summary: Configure or extend how Wolverine does the runtime (or build ahead time) code generation

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.Policies
member.summary: Options for applying conventional configuration to all or a subset of messaging endpoints

member.name: M:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.RegisterMessageType(System.Type)
member.summary: You may want to let Wolverine "know" about a message type upfront that would otherwise be discovered at runtime so that Wolverine can build in diagnostics or apply message routing upfront

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.Durability
member.summary: Advanced configuration options for Wolverine message processing, job scheduling, validation, and resiliency features and node specific settings

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.DefaultExecutionTimeout
member.summary: The default message execution timeout for local queues. This uses a CancellationTokenSource behind the scenes, and the timeout enforcement is dependent on the usage within handlers

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.DefaultRemoteInvocationTimeout
member.summary: The default remote invocation timeout over external transport. If a message is not acknowledged within the time specified, it will throw a TimeoutException

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.Services
member.summary: Register additional services to the underlying IoC container with either .NET standard IServiceCollection extension methods. This usage will have access to the application's full ServiceCollection *at the time of this call*

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.AutoBuildMessageStorageOnStartup
member.summary: Direct Wolverine to make any necessary database patches for envelope storage upon application start. Default is taken from JasperFxOptions, and is CreateOrDefault

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.ServiceName
member.summary: Descriptive name of the running service. Used in Wolverine diagnostics and testing support

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.ExternalTransportsAreStubbed
member.summary: This should probably *only* be used in development or testing to latch all outgoing message sending

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.DisableAllExternalListeners
member.summary: Should all listeners for external transports be disabled in this process? You may want to use this for command line applications that publish outbound messages

member.name: P:Wolverine.WolverineOptions.EnableAutomaticFailureAcks
member.summary: Should message failures automatically try to send a failure acknowledgement message back to the original caller. Default is *false* as of Wolverine 4.6

member.summary: Produce a report of why or why not this Wolverine application is finding or not finding methods from this handlerType USE THIS TO TROUBLESHOOT HANDLER DISCOVERY ISSUES

member.name: F:Wolverine.MultipleHandlerBehavior.ClassicCombineIntoOneLogicalHandler
member.summary: The "classic" Wolverine behavior where multiple handlers for the same message type are combined into a single logical message handler

member.name: F:Wolverine.MultipleHandlerBehavior.Separated
member.summary: Force Wolverine to make each individual handler for a message type be processed completely independently. This is common when you may be publishing an event message to multiple module handlers within the same process

member.name: F:Wolverine.WolverineMetricsMode.SystemDiagnosticsMeter
member.summary: Wolverine will publish performance metrics via System.Diagnostics.Meter where any Otel tooling can be configured to scrape metrics

member.name: F:Wolverine.WolverineMetricsMode.CritterWatch
member.summary: Wolverine will accumulate and occasionally publish performance metrics via messaging to subscribers configured to listen for Wolverine performance data

member.name: F:Wolverine.WolverineMetricsMode.Hybrid
member.summary: Wolverine will both accumulate and publish metrics information to CritterWatch *and* publish metrics via System.Diagnostics.Meter

member.name: F:Wolverine.UnknownMessageBehavior.LogOnly
member.summary: Default behavior. Wolverine will log a message that there was no known handler for the incoming message, but otherwise discard it

member.name: F:Wolverine.UnknownMessageBehavior.DeadLetterQueue
member.summary: Wolverine will log a message that there was no known handler for the incoming message, then move it to the appropriate dead letter queue for the receiving endpoint

member.name: P:Wolverine.MetricsOptions.Mode
member.summary: How should Wolverine collect and publish metrics about message handling and publications?

member.name: P:Wolverine.MetricsOptions.SamplingPeriod
member.summary: If using either CritterWatch or Hybrid metrics publishing, this is the period in which Wolverine will sample and publish metric data collection. Default is 5 seconds

member.name: T:Wolverine.IHandledTypeRule
member.summary: Extension point to help forward concrete message types to the correct message type that Wolverine handles
